conflicts.description: Chapter 6 directly shows Javier Morales killing Arvind after Tomás frames him as relay-capable, but Chapters 11 and 13 later mis-recap the death as a relay-room trap caused by a fake maintenance prompt and a sealed corridor.
conflicts.canonical_value: Arvind dies in chapter 6 when Javier, misled by Tomás's fabricated relay evidence, beats him to death with a wall sign; no sealed-corridor relay trap occurs.
conflicts.resolution_note: The chapter 6 scene is the only direct dramatization and is causally tied to Tomás's lie, Javier's panic, and Suki's immediate explanation that the relay is one-way. The later chapter 11 and 13 references read as recap drift, so the existing sheet value was retained and the recap language was not canonized.
